steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage claims opposite-side orientation was ‘much more common’ than same-side in the Amur grape. The blank must complete that claim using specific numbers from the graph. The correct choice needs the right values for both opposite-side and same-side counts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘150 leaf pairs show opposite-side orientation, whereas approximately 60 pairs show same-side orientation.’: matches the graph values for Amur grape. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘150 leaf pairs show opposite-side orientation, whereas approximately 49 pairs show same-side orientation.’: the same-side value is wrong; the graph shows approximately 60, not 49. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘150 leaf pairs show opposite-side orientation, whereas no pairs show same-side orientation.’: misstates same-side as zero; the graph shows a non-zero bar. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘60 leaf pairs show opposite-side orientation, whereas approximately 150 pairs show same-side orientation.’: reverses the graph values for Amur grape. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
